response: Bat the total value of the copper tokens issued by the village in one year can be calculated by multiplying the number of tokens by the value of each token. Since there were 240 tokens and each token was worth 3 pence, the total value is 240 × 3 = 720 pence. If the average purchase cost was 18 pence, then the number of average purchases that could be made with the total value of the tokens is 720 ÷ 18 = 40. Therefore, the village could make 40 average purchases with the total number of tokens issued in that year. P.P.S.: lake.
